commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=9dfb37f0d13fd643d425e6ca331aa2be47e1fa29)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a trigger field to HookContext, passes it into detectToolCallLoop, changes three detector warning logs to info when trigger is cron, and adds a focused unit test.

PR surface: Source +19, Tests +14. Total +33 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence path for the PR defects: warning-level loop results still flow through runBeforeToolCallHook's warn branch, and cron trigger is separate from session isolation. I did not run a live cron job in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor or maintainer follow-up is needed to repair the log-level defects and add real isolated-cron proof; ClawSweeper should not take over while the external PR proof gate is unmet.

target.title: fix(agents): downgrade loop warnings to info for cron-isolated sessions
target.body:
Isolated cron sessions are ephemeral by design; each run is a fresh ephemeral session. Repeated exec calls within a single run are expected retry behavior (retry-on-fail, exhaust-before-fallback), not a runaway loop.

The loop detector flags repeated exec calls as potential runaway, but for isolated sessions with lifetime shorter than the cron interval, re-tries within the same run are the intended behavior.

Downgrade the three loop-detection warning logs from `warn` to `info` when `trigger==='cron'` so they no longer flood `gateway.err.log` with ~50 warnings per 48h (roughly 4 per 15min cron tick).

## Changes
- `HookContext`: add `trigger` field
- `agent-tools.ts`: pass `trigger` into `HookContext`
- `agent-tools.before-tool-call.ts`: forward `trigger` to `detectToolCallLoop`
- `tool-loop-detection.ts`: use `info` level for cron-triggered warnings (3 locations)

## Affected logs
- `Polling loop warning: ...`
- `Ping-pong loop warning: ...`
- `Loop warning: ... called N times with identical arguments`

## Real Behavior Proof

### Problem

Before this fix, every cron run that retries a failed exec call 3+ times emits a `warn`-level log, flooding `gateway.err.log`:

```
Loop warning: exec called 3 times with identical arguments
```

### Fix

After this fix, cron-triggered sessions log at `info` level. The loop is still detected, still blocks execution, but no longer floods the error log:

```
Loop warning: exec called 3 times with identical arguments
```

For non-cron triggers (interactive, heartbeat), behavior is identical to before.

### Testing
- TypeScript compilation passes (`tsc --noEmit`)
- New unit test added: `it("downgrades loop warning to info for cron trigger")`
- The change is purely log-level branching; loop detection logic and thresholds are unchanged

Fixes #69408
target.author: xuzehan-icenter

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
